commands.review_summary:
Adds a `truncation-risk-latest` admin ClawScan bulk-rescan mode across the Convex API, moderator CLI, shared schemas, tests, docs, and the security moderation spec.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new admin rescan mode rather than reporting a current-main bug. Static inspection confirms current main only supports the existing `all-active-latest` mode.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-provided real behavior proof before merge; there is no narrow automation repair to queue from this review.

target.title: Add truncation-risk ClawScan rescan mode
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds an operational rescan path for a scanner-bypass class where risky content may sit past the part of `SKILL.md` that ClawScan includes in its prompt.

It does three things:

- Adds `truncation-risk-latest` to the admin bulk ClawScan rescan API and `clawhub-mod skills rescan-all`.
- Lets operators dry-run that mode to count and sample active latest skills whose primary `SKILL.md` or `skills.md` is large enough to risk prompt truncation, then queue fresh ClawScan jobs for only those artifacts.
- Updates the public/operator docs and the security moderation spec so scan results are described as current risk signals, not permanent certification or outsourced trust.

## What this solves

After a scanner rule change, prompt change, security report, or bypass disclosure, operators should not have to choose between manually rescanning individual skills and rescanning every active skill.

This PR gives them a narrower first response:

```sh
bun run mod -- skills rescan-all --mode truncation-risk-latest --dry-run --json
```

That command shows the likely scope without creating jobs. If the scope looks right, operators can rerun without `--dry-run` to queue fresh ClawScan jobs for the affected class.

## What this prevents

This helps prevent already-published, large `SKILL.md` artifacts from being left with stale scan results after ClawScan learns about a truncation-related bypass pattern. It also makes the docs clearer that a clean scan is a useful signal at a point in time, not a promise that the release is permanently safe.

## What this does not change

This does not change scanner verdict logic, hide/unhide behavior, manual moderation decisions, or install gating. The new mode only selects existing published artifacts for fresh ClawScan jobs.

## Tests

- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run convex/securityScan.test.ts`
- `(cd packages/clawhub-mod && ../../node_modules/.bin/vitest run -c vitest.config.ts src/commands/moderation.test.ts)`
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run packages/schema/src/schemas.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/tsc -p packages/clawhub-mod/tsconfig.json --noEmit && ./node_modules/.bin/tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bun run --cwd packages/schema build`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check convex/securityScan.ts convex/securityScan.test.ts convex/httpApiV1/skillsV1.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/cli.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/commands/moderation.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/commands/moderation.test.ts packages/schema/src/schemas.ts packages/clawhub/src/schema/schemas.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --type-aware --tsconfig ./tsconfig.oxlint.json convex/securityScan.ts convex/securityScan.test.ts convex/httpApiV1/skillsV1.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/cli.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/commands/moderation.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/commands/moderation.test.ts packages/schema/src/schemas.ts packages/clawhub/src/schema/schemas.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Codex `/review` was not run, per request.
